TNBC Logic-Gating Target Discovery — Results Report

Triple-Negative Breast Cancer · cell-surface antigen + environmental protease (two layers) · specificity & coverage scored on separate axes · logic synthesis from orthogonality
Headline result
For TNBC we recommend (EPCAM OR VTCN1) AND MMP11 — malignant-cell coverage 51%, safety 0.96, final score 0.83. The OR of two surface antigens widens tumor-cell coverage (47% for EPCAM alone → 51–56%); the AND-MMP11 protease gate is near-zero in the organs EPCAM leaks into (kidney, pancreas, lung) yet high in TNBC, restoring specificity. The logic is modality-independent — the same (A OR B) AND protease rule can be built as a logic-gated CAR-T cell or as a pro-antibody molecule that only becomes active when both surface antigens and the protease are present.

1. Approach

Cancer therapy faces two problems that a single target rarely solves at once — tumor specificity (hit the tumor, spare normal organs) and coverage of tumor heterogeneity. We score cell-surface antigens ("address") and microenvironmental proteases ("permit") on independent axes, compute cell- and tissue-level orthogonality, and automatically recommend logic gates. Because specificity and coverage trade off against each other (AND raises specificity but lowers coverage; OR does the reverse), the axes are stored separately and recombined only at the completed-logic level.

2. Data & scale

2,886 surface candidates (SURFY surfaceome) → narrowed to 56 30 proteases (literature) TCGA Basal 171 patients TNBC single-cell 161,048 cells 1,557 logics scored
How candidates were selected
· Surface (2,886): the full SURFY surfaceome (human proteins with experimentally/ML-confirmed surface localization) — a tumor-agnostic candidate pool.
· Surface → 56: genes passing three gates (tumor expression ≥ 30 TPM AND interpatient coverage ≥ 40% [>100 TPM] AND spec_score ≥ 0.5) → top 45 by a rank-blended pick score + 11 forced validation anchors (EGFR, MET, FOLH1, MSLN, MUC1, TACSTD2, ERBB2, EPCAM, CD276, MUC16, VTCN1) → 56 after de-duplication. Narrowed first because single-cell co-expression is computationally expensive.
· Proteases (30): of the ~550–600 human degradome, only tumor-associated proteases with reported extracellular activity were curated (MMP ×10, serine ×10, cysteine ×4, ADAM ×3, ADAMTS/aspartic/glycosidase ×1 each).
Data sources
· Normal tissue: GTEx, 54 tissues, median TPM (safety / tissue orthogonality).
· Tumor expression: cBioPortal TCGA-BRCA PanCancer Atlas, PAM50 Basal-like (n=171) as a TNBC proxy (TCGA lacks IHC receptor status; Basal-like overlaps TNBC ~80%).
· Single-cell: CELLxGENE Census, real "triple-negative breast carcinoma" cells (161,048; 48,862 malignant) — cell orthogonality & intratumoral coverage.
· Comparable-scale heatmap: UCSC Xena TOIL (GTEx + TCGA reprocessed on one TPM pipeline).

3. Per-axis scoring — surface layer

Three axes are stored separately: specificity (tumor vs normal), intratumoral coverage (% malignant cells, single-cell), and interpatient coverage (% patients, TCGA). Top candidates matched known TNBC targets.

How each column is computed (input → function → output)
ColumnInputFunctionOutput
spec_score
(bulk-organ specificity)		 tumor expression (TCGA Basal median RSEM) · normal-tissue burden tox_load = Σ(GTEx TPM × tissue-criticality weight) 0.45 × pctrank(log1p tumor) + 0.55 × (1 − pctrank(log1p tox_load)); rank transform absorbs the platform difference 0–1. higher = high tumor expression & low normal risk (organ-level specificity)
inter_cov_pct_patients
(interpatient coverage)		 per-patient tumor expression across the 171 Basal patients % of patients with expression > 100 TPM (antigen-density threshold) 0–100%. what fraction of patients express the target
intra_cov_pct_cells
(intratumoral coverage)		 single-cell counts across 48,862 malignant TNBC cells % of malignant cells with count > 0 0–100%. a dropout-limited lower bound
sc_tumor_cell_specificity
(tumor-cell specificity)		 malignant detection rate vs max non-malignant cell-type detection rate (single-cell) malignant ÷ (malignant + max non-malignant) 0–1. →1 means expressed only on tumor cells
spec_score vs sc_tumor_cell_specificity — the first compares tumor tissue vs normal organs (bulk; "is the target quiet across the body?"), the second compares malignant vs non-malignant cells within the same tumor (single-cell; "does it bind only tumor cells, or also normal epithelium?"). Complementary: whole-body specificity vs within-tumor specificity. e.g. EPCAM is 46.8% on malignant cells but higher (67.8%) on intratumoral normal epithelium → cell specificity 0.41.
Why 100 TPM: 10 TPM only means "transcript detected"; it does not reflect the antigen density a CAR-T needs to form a lytic synapse (most candidates saturate near 100%, so it has no discriminating power). 100 TPM approximates the effective recognition density — validation targets (ERBB2, EPCAM, TACSTD2) still exceed 98%, while low expressers drop honestly. Criticality weights: brain/heart/liver/lung/kidney = 3.0, pancreas/blood = 2.5, … breast/skin/adipose = 1.0, cultured lines = 0.5, default = 1.5.
Intratumoral coverage is decisive for surface targets. Most candidates reach ~100% interpatient coverage (light bars), but tumor-cell detection (dark green) peaks at EPCAM 46.8% and clusters at 20–35%. A CAR-T misses antigen-negative tumor cells, so even when most patients express the target, escape occurs if only some malignant cells within a tumor do — this motivates widening coverage with an OR gate (§5–6).
Genespec_score
bulk-organ spec.		inter_cov_pct_patients
>100 TPM		inter_cov (z>1)
vs normal breast		intra_cov_pct_cellssc_tumor_cell_specificity
tumor-cell spec.

EGFR0.5198.26.421.10.37
Two interpatient-coverage columns: >100 TPM is "% of patients with CAR-recognizable antigen density"; z>1 is "% of patients over-expressing vs normal breast". The former stays high for validation targets (>98%), the latter isolates true tumor-specific over-expression — e.g. EGFR/ERBB2 have sufficient density but only ~6% over-express vs normal breast. The narrowing gate uses the >100 TPM column.

4. Per-axis scoring — protease layer

Proteases play a gating role, so intratumoral coverage is not applicable — the enzyme need not be expressed by every tumor cell, only be active in the microenvironment. Lacking public activity data, we use expression as an activity proxy, penalized by same-family inhibitor expression (TIMP/SERPIN/cystatin).

inhibitor_penalty
A protease can be expressed yet inhibited by abundant endogenous inhibitors in the TME. As a net-activity proxy we use the protease's relative amount vs its inhibitors:
penalty = inhibitor expression ÷ (protease + inhibitor), both at TME median RSEM (TCGA Basal 171). →1 when inhibitors dominate (activity suppressed), lower when the protease keeps pace. Applied as × (1 − 0.25 × penalty). This reflects net activity by actual expression ratio, not mere presence/absence. Aspartic (CTSD) and glycosidase (HPSE) have no matching inhibitor family (penalty 0).
Why a 250 TPM coverage threshold (vs 10 TPM for surface)
A protease is an enzyme that opens the gate by cleaving substrate, so it needs enough activity density to actually open the gate, not mere detection. At 50 TPM most top candidates saturate near 100% with no discrimination; at 250 TPM genuine high expressers (ST14, ADAM10/ADAM17, MMP11) separate from low expressers that cannot open a gate (HPSE 4%, MMP16 10%, MMP13 39%). The threshold applies to coverage only — the specificity axis is a normal-tissue ratio.
Proteasefamilyspec_score_ACTIVITY_PROXYinter_cov_pct_patients
>250 TPM		spec_tme_tpminhibitor_penalty
MMP11MMP0.7097.12,5140.87
ADAM12ADAM0.6690.19850.95
MMP13MMP0.7438.61740.99
MMP1MMP0.5864.34960.97
MMP7MMP0.5390.64,1100.81
PLAUserine0.4598.81,6240.87
FAPserine0.4177.25700.95
ST14serine0.52100.05,2460.68
Activity-proxy caveat — expression ≠ enzyme activity. Approximated by normal-tissue expression ratio and inhibitor burden; true activity needs zymography / activity-based probes.

5. Orthogonality — the basis for logic design

Why gating is needed — focused view. The five surface antigens (top) are high in TNBC but leak into normal organs — EPCAM reaches kidney 83, pancreas 53, lung 51 TPM. A single CAR would target those organs (on-target off-tumor toxicity). The gate proteases (bottom) are quiet in exactly those organs yet active in TNBC — MMP11 is kidney 1, pancreas 0, lung 1 but TNBC 51, ideal. MMP7 is kidney 35, so it cannot block kidney leak (protease choice matters). Thus (surface OR) AND (protease) widens tumor-cell coverage while the protease excludes normal organs — coverage and safety together. Values use UCSC Xena TOIL (GTEx + TCGA reprocessed on one TPM scale) so tumor and normal are directly comparable.
Gate-protease selection — housekeeping excluded. A gate must be "on only in the tumor environment"; a housekeeping sheddase like ADAM10, expressed in nearly all normal tissues, has no gating value. On TOIL comparable TPM: ADAM10 is active in 21 normal tissues (Gini 0.31, uniform), ADAM17/ST14 also broad → excluded. FAP is local to few tissues (Gini 0.63) so it is usable if those are avoided; MMP11 is active in only one normal tissue (best). The filter drops proteases active in >8 tissues or Gini < 0.4, leaving gate proteases MMP11, MMP7, ADAM12, FAP, PLAU.

Cell orthogonality (single-cell)

Whether a surface pair is expressed on the same malignant cell (favors AND) or on different cells (OR raises coverage), judged by a co-expression matrix. e.g. EPCAM alone 47% → EPCAM OR TACSTD2 56% (coverage up), while AND drops to 25% (specificity up, escape up).

Tissue orthogonality (GTEx / TOIL)

Normal organs a surface target leaks into are blocked by a protease absent there but active in TNBC — the core of the 3-input gate.

6. Recommended logic gates

How the logic-table columns are computed
ColumnInputFunction
coveragesingle-cell malignant co-expression (48,862 malignant cells)% of malignant cells the logic switches on. single = detection rate; OR = either gene on (union); AND = both on (intersection). A gate uses the inner OR's coverage (the protease gates by tissue, not by cell).
safetyGTEx 54-tissue expression + tissue criticality→1 with less normal-organ leak. leak_burden = Σ criticality(t)×log1p(leak TPM) over tissues >10 TPM. safety = 1 − norm(leak_burden). A gate adds up to +0.4 in proportion to the critical leak organs the protease silences.
final_scorethe three above + complexity= 0.55×norm(coverage) + 0.45×safety − 0.05×(inputs−1). Coverage and safety dominate; circuit complexity is a small penalty. (An earlier escape term was dropped — escape ≈ 1−coverage, redundant with coverage.)
coverage and safety move in opposite directions (OR: coverage↑ safety↓; AND: the reverse), so they are kept separate and combined only at the completed-logic level. A gate (A OR B) AND P secures coverage via the OR and blocks normal-organ leak via protease P — lifting both at once.
Logictypen_inputscoveragesafetyfinal_score

(DSC2 OR EPCAM) AND MMP11gate (A OR B) AND P351%0.930.82
(GPR180 OR EPCAM) AND MMP11gate (A OR B) AND P349%0.950.80
(SLC26A2 OR EPCAM) AND MMP11gate (A OR B) AND P352%0.880.80
(MSLN OR EPCAM) AND MMP11gate (A OR B) AND P350%0.920.80
(TACSTD2 OR EPCAM) AND MMP11gate (A OR B) AND P356%0.760.79
EPCAMsingle147%0.560.71
EPCAM OR VTCN1OR251%0.560.70
Two lead recommendations
Protease-only (single-input) gating is not possible — a protease is an environmental signal that cannot point at a cell and is not a CAR binding target (§5). Every gate is at least (surface antigen) AND (protease).

· ① Coverage-first — (TACSTD2 OR EPCAM) AND MMP11 (coverage 56% · final 0.79): TACSTD2 (TROP2) + EPCAM give the highest malignant-cell coverage (56%), with the protease gating the leak organs (kidney, lung, pancreas). Both anchors are validated clinical targets (sacituzumab; multiple EPCAM CARs), so this is the most feasible. Safety is lower (0.76) because TACSTD2 is expressed on several epithelia.
· ② Safety-first — (EPCAM OR VTCN1) AND MMP11 (safety 0.96 · final 0.83): VTCN1 (B7-H4) leaks almost nowhere in normal organs, giving top safety; coverage is slightly lower (51%). This ranks #1 by final_score.

· Tissue gate (AND protease): blocks the critical organs the OR leaks into. The proteases that actually build gates = MMP11, MMP7, ADAM12, FAP, PLAU. Housekeeping proteases (ADAM10 in 21 tissues, ADAM17 uniform, ST14 in 13 tissues) have no gating value and were excluded (§5); MMP13/HPSE fail the 250 TPM coverage cut. Proteases blocking the same organs score nearly identically, so pick by activity-proxy specificity.
MMP11 (stromelysin-3) is nearly absent from normal tissue (active in one) and specific to breast-cancer stroma — the best gate. It ties with ADAM12/FAP on score (interchangeable) but has the highest tumor specificity.

7. Limitations & next steps

  • Basal ≈ TNBC proxy: TCGA lacks IHC receptor status, so PAM50 Basal-like (~80% overlap) is used.
  • Modality: transcript-based — CAR-T recognizes surface protein, so top candidates need protein-level (flow/IHC) validation.
  • Protease activity: an expression proxy — needs enzymatic-activity confirmation.
  • Single-cell dropout: intratumoral coverage is a detection-rate lower bound.
  • Scope (pilot): this is a pilot — 56 surface candidates (score gate) and 30 curated proteases. The same method extends to the full surfaceome (2,886) and full degradome (~550–600 proteases), where more logic combinations and untested targets would emerge (single-cell co-expression cost is the main constraint).
  • Next: expand to full surfaceome/degradome; protein-level confirmation of top logics; protease-activated CAR / SynNotch circuit design; in vitro validation.
